headline.text: L3 ML4 *scratched Sweco screen* caught at 21:00, replaced with no material found in downstream product — L1 S3 C1 roller bearing flagged for FM risk, serviced preemptively.

summary: Three food-safety events: L3 ML4 scratched chocolate filter screen replaced after immediate stop-and-swap; L1 S3 grinding belt roller serviced before failure; L2 ML3 roof leak isolated from product zone. L3 ML1 ran out of tempering spec overnight with mixing water 1.3°F below centerline minimum.

food_safety.title: Scratched Sweco screen on ML4 north side
food_safety.summary: Chocolate pumping stopped immediately at 21:00 when operator found scratched filter screen; screen replaced, magnet inspected, no material observed coming off screen or in product since last good check at 20:00.
food_safety.status: Resolved — screen replaced, no contamination found; root cause investigation ongoing (suspected heavy flow from primary left discharge pipe).

food_safety.title: S3 C1 belt roller bearing grinding, FM risk flagged
food_safety.summary: Post-metal-detector belt roller making grinding noise; maintenance found no metal shavings or FM but flagged bearing-failure contamination risk; bearing removed, greased, reinstalled pending Monday replacement.
food_safety.status: Resolved — line back up at 04:30; new bearings arrive Monday; no evidence of contamination during inspection.

food_safety.title: ML3 roof leak dripping near product zone
food_safety.summary: Water dripping from RTU above Z belt and supersack hopper; line paused, supersack removed, vent sock disconnected to isolate hopper while roof drain cleared; no water contact with product or equipment.
food_safety.status: Resolved — vent sock reconnected 05:04; William confirmed drain pan adjusted and no further drip risk.

quality.title: ML1 mixing water 1.3°F below centerline minimum
quality.summary: Tempering instability overnight; mixing water dropped to 79.7°F at 21:19 (centerline minimum 81.0°F), causing phase-4 crystal formation and high temper reads (0.8–0.9); cold water had been at 57°F for days before recipe loaded.
quality.status: Open — team corrected settings during 3rd shift; Robert Villarreal coaching on centerline adherence and pre-cooling control.

quality.title: ML2 Sweco loosened and spilled chocolate
quality.summary: Sweco screen not tightened after assembly, moved during startup and spilled chocolate; depositing stopped, Sweco adjusted, temper allowed to stabilize before restart.
quality.status: Resolved — depositing resumed 06:13 after Sweco secured and temper recovery.

opportunities.text: Flag S3 C1 roller bearing work as strong example of proactive FM risk mitigation — William and Caleb identified grinding noise, assessed contamination risk, and serviced before failure.

Consider adding Sweco screen visual inspection to ML4 startup checklist — today's scratched screen and Angel's observation that primary left discharge pipe delivers heaviest flow suggest routine inspection could catch wear earlier.
Review recipe changeover procedure for ML1 tempering unit settings — cold water remained at 57°F for days before today's recipe load, contributing to overnight tempering instability.
